title: French Social Democracy: The Fourth Republic
author: Autumn Chen, modded by munt

options.title: Élections législatives de 1946
options.subtitle: The first National Assembly of the Fourth Republic is to be elected.

content.content: The first National Assembly elections of the Fourth Republic are to be held now.

tripartite_sfio.title: Form an SFIO-led Tripartite government.
tripartite_sfio.subtitle:
PCF + SFIO + MRP (457/627 | 72.9%).
The Communists and Christian democrats are willing to preserve Tripartisme under a Socialist compromise.

content: The mutual veto of the
and
leaves the
as the only acceptable intermediary. Paul Ramadier is invested as President of the Council.

The restored Tripartite cabinet commands 457 seats. Maurice Thorez returns as vice-president of the Council, and the Communists retain major responsibility for labour, reconstruction and social policy. The
remains in government rather than permit a ministry dependent on the two workers’ parties alone.
The majority is enormous; its programme is not. Wage demands, the price freeze, denominational education, Indochina and relations with the Soviet Union all divide the partners. The
has obtained the premiership because each larger party fears the other more than it distrusts us. Nothing in the election result gives Ramadier independent authority over either.

homogeneous_socialist.title: Form a homogeneous Socialist provisional government.
homogeneous_socialist.subtitle: SFIO (102/627 | 16.3%). The PCF and MRP accept a five-week caretaker after their own candidates fail; Blum pledges to resign once the presidency is installed.

content: Maurice Thorez receives only 259 votes, and Georges Bidault cannot assemble a majority either. Vincent Auriol therefore turns to Léon Blum.
Blum is invested by 575 of the 583 deputies voting. That vote is not an endorsement of an

programme. It is an agreement that he should end the governmental deadlock and complete the transition to the permanent institutions of the Fourth Republic. When he cannot reconstruct Tripartisme, he presents an entirely Socialist cabinet on 17 December.
The
and

do not bring the cabinet down because its limited remit and its end date are understood in advance: it will govern until the President of the Republic is elected, then resign. Neither larger party gains anything from reopening the failed coalition negotiations for a government expected to last only five weeks.
In that interval the cabinet approves the Monnet Plan, announces a five per cent reduction in prices and confronts the outbreak of war in Hanoi.

thorez_tripartite.title: Entrust Tripartisme to Maurice Thorez of the PCF?
thorez_tripartite.subtitle: PCF + SFIO + MRP (457/627 | 72.9%).
thorez_tripartite.unavailableSubtitle:
PCF + SFIO + MRP (457/627 | 72.9%).
The MRP will not accept a Communist President of the Council, and twenty-five of our own deputies refuse to vote for Thorez. Even unanimous SFIO support would leave him short without the Christian democrats.

workers_front.title: Form a Front ouvrier?
workers_front.subtitle: PCF + SFIO (284/627 | 45.3%).
workers_front.unavailableSubtitle:
PCF + SFIO (284/627 | 45.3%).
We lack the votes necessary to form France’s most left-wing cabinet in history. The United States would disapprove of such a left-wing government. As if we care what they think.

popular_front.title: Re-create the Front populaire?
popular_front.subtitle: PCF + SFIO + RGR (353/627 | 56.3%).
popular_front.unavailableSubtitle:
PCF + SFIO + RGR (353/627 | 56.3%).
The Radicals and UDSR will not enter a Communist-dominated cabinet. Oh, and Hitler is dead. Didn't anyone tell you? Not much point for an anti-fascist coalition if fascism is, well, dead.

third_force.title: Break with the PCF and form a Third Force coalition, as though the August Congress had not happened?
third_force.subtitle: SFIO + MRP + RGR + Moderates (416/627 | 66.3%).
third_force.unavailableSubtitle:
SFIO + MRP + RGR + Moderates (416/627 | 66.3%).
The August Congress has only just rejected Daniel Mayer’s leadership and brought Guy Mollet to power on a call for unity of action with the Communists. Entering an anti-Communist coalition now would require the new majority to repudiate itself before it has even taken office.

republican_coalition.title: Refuse to form a government, letting an MRP-led Republican Coalition form.
republican_coalition.subtitle:
MRP + RGR + Moderates + Others (343/627 | 54.7%).
Really? After all we have done to keep the Gaullists and the old parliamentary right from taking charge of the new Republic, we are going to... let them into government?

content: Georges Bidault of the
remains at the head of government.
The Christian democrats combine with the
, the Republican independents and the parliamentary right to reach 343 seats. The
returns to opposition rather than continue mediating between the two larger parties.
The majority is sufficient, but its basis is negative. The Radicals remain anticlerical; the

defends the social and educational settlement of the Resistance; the conservatives want a firmer retreat from nationalisation and the institutions of the Liberation. Mollet may attack the coalition from the left. He cannot conceal that our refusal supplied its majority.

content.content: Election figures and institutional chronology are based chiefly on the French National Assembly’s historical material and contemporary parliamentary group totals.
Assemblée nationale: The Constitution of 1946 and establishment of the Fourth Republic
